antiReferences.name: Gym-bro protein RTDs
antiReferences.why: They make protein feel like performance, discipline, and body optimization. PEP in this direction must make protein feel social, refreshing, and desirable out of home.

antiReferences.name: drinkpep.com
antiReferences.why: Name collision only. That product is an unrelated US energy/probiotic drink and must not be treated as the same brand, product, tone, or positioning.

antiReferences.name: PROTOCHOL
antiReferences.why: Although it combines protein with social drinking, the alcohol-plus-protein novelty risks making PEP feel gimmicky and confusing. PEP should be alcohol-free social ritual, not a protein-alcohol hybrid.

antiReferences.name: Clinical functional hydration brands
antiReferences.why: They over-index on utility, claims, and ingredient logic. PEP needs emotional ordering power in hospitality and social settings.

antiReferences.name: Kids’ fruity soft drinks
antiReferences.why: PEP can be fruity and fun, but it must stay adult, premium, and occasion-worthy.

audience: Social, aesthetic, experience-led consumers who want something better to order when they are out.
People reducing alcohol or choosing alcohol-free options without wanting to feel excluded from the occasion.
Hospitality guests at bars, restaurants, hotels, festivals, dinners, dates, and rooftop events.
Consumers who are curious about protein benefits but do not want a gym-coded drink in social moments.
People who choose drinks emotionally: taste, ritual, packaging presence, and how it feels in the group.
brandFitRules: Lead with social pleasure, taste, and occasion before nutrition.
Protein is the feel-good twist, not the headline personality.
Reject anything that feels gym-first, clinical, medicinal, or performance-obsessed.
Design and copy must feel natural in bars, restaurants, hotels, and festivals.
Keep the tone premium, fun, adult, and refreshing.
Do not imitate alcohol brands directly; borrow ritual confidence, not category clichés.
If the work does not support “I order this when I go out,” it is off direction.

designPatterns: Premium presence with restrained information density
Functional benefits framed as experience rather than optimization
inLineBrands.drinkTypeFit: Strong social drink fit. Not protein-led, but directly competes for alcohol-free social occasions and premium ordering moments.
inLineBrands.name: Kin Euphorics
inLineBrands.why: Kin proves that functional benefits can be made socially desirable when the brand leads with mood, ritual, and occasion instead of technical nutrition.

designPatterns: Range architecture built around occasions and serves
inLineBrands.drinkTypeFit: Strong social occasion fit. The format is spirit-adjacent rather than protein refreshment, so PEP should learn from the ritual but avoid direct imitation.
inLineBrands.name: Lyre's
inLineBrands.why: Lyre’s is a leading benchmark for making alcohol-free choices feel credible, adult, and socially accepted in hospitality.

inLineBrands.drinkTypeFit: Strong alcohol-free social benchmark. Less direct as a ready-to-drink protein beverage, but highly relevant for premium social credibility.
inLineBrands.name: Seedlip
inLineBrands.why: Seedlip established that non-alcoholic drinks can have premium pricing power and social status when built around ritual and adult taste.

inLineBrands.drinkTypeFit: Very strong order-choice fit for alcohol-free social settings. Not protein-led, but competes directly against the boring soft drink problem.
inLineBrands.name: Royal Club Mocktails
inLineBrands.why: Royal Club Mocktails sits close to the exact PEP occasion: an adult, non-alcoholic drink choice for people who want something more exciting than standard soft drinks.

inLineBrands.drinkTypeFit: Strong social soft-drink and mixer fit. Weaker as a full PEP analogue because it is not protein-led or flavour-range-led in the same way.
inLineBrands.name: Royal Club Ginger Ale
inLineBrands.why: It demonstrates how a soft drink can feel adult and socially acceptable in bar contexts without relying on heavy functional claims.

layoutOnlyReferences.name: Revized
patterns: Use only for page structure inspiration if needed: strong product-grid clarity, quick flavour navigation, and modular sections.
Do not borrow its brand tone, ingredient hierarchy, or visual identity.

oneLiner: PEP is the premium protein drink made for going out: refreshing, fruity, social, and alcohol-free.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Weak for this direction: functional vitamin drink rather than social hospitality drink.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: HOLY
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Belongs to functional protein drink direction.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: It leads with functional health and protein utility, which would pull PEP toward routine and supplementation rather than social ordering.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Weak: hydration mix rather than ready social drink.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: LMNT
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Belongs to functional protein drink direction.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Its logic is electrolyte replenishment and dietary function, not pleasure, belonging, or out-of-home ritual.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Weak: BCAA/energy drink for sport and recovery.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: NOCCO
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Belongs to functional protein drink direction.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: It is too performance-led and gym-adjacent for PEP’s better social drink direction.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Partial but problematic: protein plus alcohol creates social relevance, but the alcohol fusion conflicts with PEP’s alcohol-free better-option role.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: PROTOCHOL
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Belongs mainly to functional protein drink direction with novelty social crossover.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: It risks making protein in a social context feel strange or gimmicky, which is the main risk PEP must avoid.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Weak: functional hydration rather than premium social drink.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: VITAMIN WELL
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Belongs to functional protein drink direction.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: It frames the purchase around vitamins and hydration, not social experience or hospitality pricing power.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Moderate: refreshing soda format, but wellness gut-health logic dominates.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: BLOOM
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: It is more about healthier everyday soda replacement than premium out-of-home social ritual.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Moderate: non-alcoholic aperitif/soft drink can support social moments, but its main lane is natural lifestyle upgrade.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: EVER TREE
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: It is useful as inspiration but would pull PEP toward botanical aperitif sophistication rather than fruity protein refreshment.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Moderate: soda replacement, but wellness benefit is primary.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: OLIPOP
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: It competes in everyday gut-health soda, not premium bar, restaurant, and festival ordering.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Moderate: fun soda energy, but mostly an everyday health-conscious soft drink.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Poppi
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: It is too tied to prebiotic soda and daily lifestyle wellness to define PEP’s social hospitality role.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Moderate: kombucha can act as a premium soft drink alternative, but fermentation and craft wellness dominate.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Yaya Kombucha
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Unassigned in source; closest to lifestyle/craft wellness rather than active social protein direction.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: It is refreshing and premium, but its credibility comes from organic kombucha craft rather than social protein ritual.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Moderate: craft alternative to alcohol or soda, but kombucha/botanical fermentation is the main frame.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Walker Brothers
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Unassigned in source; closest to lifestyle/craft wellness rather than active social protein direction.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Its craft and probiotic cues would move PEP away from accessible premium social refreshment.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Moderate: refreshing kombucha for lifestyle moments, but not a premium hospitality-first drink.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Alive & Well
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: It is health and wellness-led, whereas PEP social should be occasion and experience-led.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Moderate: alcohol-free canned refreshment, but relaxation and wellness are primary.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Recess
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: It is more about calm everyday wellness than celebratory out-of-home ritual.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Partial: festive ready-to-drink cocktails and mocktails overlap with social occasions.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Ole cocktails
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Belongs to lifestyle upgrade direction in source.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Useful for party energy, but cocktail and alcohol associations could obscure PEP’s own protein-refreshment point of difference.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Partial: strong mindful drinking and dinner-party relevance.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Ish
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Belongs to lifestyle upgrade direction in source.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: It is closer to imitation spirits and mocktails than to PEP’s fruity protein drink proposition.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Moderate: functional soda can be refreshing, but the purchase logic is health-forward.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: more fiz
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Its sugar-free and fiber-enriched logic would pull PEP toward wellness claims instead of social desire.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.drinkTypeFit: Strong product-format fit for protein refreshment, weak social-positioning fit.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Juuz
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.positioningMismatch: Belongs to functional protein drink direction.
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: It is highly relevant to product feasibility, but its active direction is protein utility rather than premium social ritual.

positioningFitTypeMismatch.drinkTypeFit: Strong occasion and flavour fit, but cider is not PEP’s alcohol-free protein refreshment format.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.name: Rekorderlig
positioningFitTypeMismatch.positioningMismatch: Matches the better social drink mindset, but sits in cider rather than protein drink.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.typeMismatch: Alcoholic or cider-coded fruit refreshment; not PEP’s non-alcoholic protein-led proposition.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.whyBrandingFits: Useful for fruit-forward premium social refreshment, festival and outdoor drinking energy, and making flavour feel like the reason to order.

positioningFitTypeMismatch.drinkTypeFit: Strong party and outdoor-event fit, but cocktail logic is not PEP’s core.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.name: Ole cocktails
positioningFitTypeMismatch.positioningMismatch: Source places it in lifestyle upgrade, with social overlap.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.typeMismatch: Cocktail and alcohol association can overpower PEP’s protein refreshment difference.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.whyBrandingFits: Useful for celebratory convenience, flavour-led serving, and social energy.

positioningFitTypeMismatch.drinkTypeFit: Strong mindful social fit, but spirit and mocktail mimicry is not PEP’s main role.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.name: Ish
positioningFitTypeMismatch.positioningMismatch: Source places it in lifestyle upgrade, with strong social overlap.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.typeMismatch: Imitation spirit/cocktail format differs from PEP’s own protein drink format.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.whyBrandingFits: Useful for premium alcohol-free confidence, dinner-party relevance, and adult inclusion.

positioningFitTypeMismatch.drinkTypeFit: Moderate social fit, weaker protein-drink fit.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.name: EVER TREE
positioningFitTypeMismatch.positioningMismatch: Source places it in lifestyle upgrade rather than active social direction.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.typeMismatch: Aperitif and botanical soft drink cues could make PEP feel like a different category.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.whyBrandingFits: Useful for sophisticated alcohol-free occasions and premium non-alcoholic serve culture.

positioningFitTypeMismatch.drinkTypeFit: Moderate: can replace alcohol or sugary soda, but fermentation and craft cues dominate.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.name: Walker Brothers
positioningFitTypeMismatch.positioningMismatch: Unassigned in source; closer to craft wellness than PEP social.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.typeMismatch: Kombucha/probiotic craft format is different from light protein refreshment.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.whyBrandingFits: Useful for adult complexity, craft credibility, and alternative social drinking mindset.

positioningFitTypeMismatch.drinkTypeFit: Moderate: premium refreshment and mixer potential, but kombucha category logic differs.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.name: Yaya Kombucha
positioningFitTypeMismatch.positioningMismatch: Unassigned in source; closer to premium wellness/craft.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.typeMismatch: Kombucha and fermentation cues are not central to PEP’s protein-led offer.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.whyBrandingFits: Useful for premium, authentic, refreshing soft-drink alternatives with flavour variety.

positioningStatement: PEP is the premium protein drink for going out: an alcohol-free, flavour-led social ritual that helps people feel good and have fun without choosing a boring soft drink.

visual.designConcept: fun, premium packaging, vibrant, festival vibe, fruity
visual.mood: Warm, energetic, sociable, sensory, premium-but-approachable, playful without becoming childish, and made for shared out-of-home moments.
visual.photography: Show PEP in real social contexts: hands holding drinks, groups arriving, tables at dinner, festival moments, dates, rooftops, hotel bars, and close-up serving rituals. Photography should feel candid, tactile, flavour-led, and socially alive. Avoid gym scenes, solo nutrition routines, clinical ingredient setups, and isolated product shots that feel like supplement advertising.
rejectLooks: Gym supplement layouts with dense claims and performance cues.
Clinical wellness layouts that make the drink feel medicinal.
Overly quiet lifestyle minimalism that removes social energy.
Direct imitation of spirit, wine, beer, or cocktail brands.
Overcrowded packaging that treats every benefit as equally important.

voice.tone: Confident, social, playful, premium, flavour-led, and concise. Copy should sound like an invitation to join the moment, not a nutrition lecture.
